Each phase (R, I, V) has a distinct configurable deadline rather than a single global wall-clock limit.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_RESEARCH_TIMEOUT and SPIRAL_IMPL_TIMEOUT env vars configure per-phase deadlines in seconds (defaults: 300, 600) alongside the existing SPIRAL_VALIDATE_TIMEOUT
Each LLM call is wrapped: `timeout --kill-after=30 "$PHASE_TIMEOUT" <llm_command>`
Exit code 124 from timeout is caught and logged as a phase_timeout event in spiral_events.jsonl with the phase name and story ID
A timed-out phase is treated as a failure (increments retry count) not a hard abort
All three per-phase timeouts (R, I, V) are applied to their respective LLM calls
technicalNotes: Use `timeout --kill-after=30` (SIGTERM first, then SIGKILL after 30s) to allow graceful LLM CLI shutdown
Test on macOS with `gtimeout` (from coreutils) and Linux `timeout`; auto-detect in setup/doctor
Per-phase timeouts must survive the existing retry loop — a timeout retry still respects the backoff delay
Log timeout events with phase, story_id, timeout_secs for post-run analysis

userStories.title: Add prd.json JSON Schema file and validation as CI preflight gate
userStories.description: Define a JSON Schema for prd.json and validate it before every SPIRAL run and in CI. This catches malformed PRDs (missing required fields, wrong types, invalid priority values) before an LLM wastes API budget on a broken input.
acceptanceCriteria: A schema file prd.schema.json is committed to the repo root defining required story fields: id (string, pattern US-\d+), title (string maxLength 80), priority (enum: critical/high/medium/low), description (string), acceptanceCriteria (array of strings, minItems 1), passes (boolean)
spiral.sh runs `python -m jsonschema -i prd.json prd.schema.json` (or equivalent) as an early preflight step
Validation failure prints a diff-style error listing which story and field failed, then aborts with exit 2
A GitHub Actions workflow step validates prd.json on every PR that modifies prd.json
The --doctor flag checks that python jsonschema is importable and prints install instructions if missing
technicalNotes: Use python jsonschema library rather than adding a new binary
The schema should use additionalProperties: false on story objects to catch typos in field names
Allow extra top-level prd.json fields (productName, goals, etc.) but strictly validate each element of userStories array
Consider a jq-only fallback: `jq -e '[.userStories[] | select(.id == null or .title == null)] | length == 0'`

userStories.title: Add per-story token cost accumulation and soft/hard budget enforcement
userStories.description: Track cumulative LLM token usage and estimated cost per story by parsing Claude CLI usage output. Emit a cost_update event to spiral_events.jsonl after each LLM call. Enforce a per-story soft limit (warn) and hard limit (skip story) using SPIRAL_STORY_COST_WARN_USD and SPIRAL_STORY_COST_HARD_USD.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh parses token counts from LLM CLI JSON output after each call and accumulates them in .spiral/story_costs.json keyed by story_id
A cost_update JSONL event is emitted after each LLM call with fields: story_id, tokens_input, tokens_output, estimated_usd
When cumulative story cost exceeds SPIRAL_STORY_COST_WARN_USD (default: $0.50), a warning is printed but execution continues
When cumulative story cost exceeds SPIRAL_STORY_COST_HARD_USD (default: $2.00), the story is abandoned with _failureReason: story_cost_ceiling
The --status flag shows total run cost accumulated across all stories from story_costs.json
technicalNotes: This is per-story cost, complementing the existing SPIRAL_COST_CEILING which is a session-level hard stop
Model pricing constants (input $/1M tokens, output $/1M tokens) should be defined in spiral.config.example.sh and overridable
story_costs.json must be written atomically to survive worker crashes
If the LLM CLI does not emit token counts, log tokens_input: null and skip cost estimation rather than blocking

userStories.title: Add model fallback chain with per-model circuit breaker state
userStories.description: When the primary LLM provider's circuit breaker is OPEN, automatically rotate to a secondary model from a configured fallback chain before giving up on a story. Each model in the chain maintains its own circuit breaker state so a fully degraded provider does not contaminate fallbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_MODEL_FALLBACK_CHAIN env var accepts a colon-separated list of model identifiers (e.g., `claude-sonnet-4-5:claude-haiku-3-5:gemini-2.0-flash`)
ralph.sh attempts each model in the chain in order; a model is skipped if its circuit breaker is OPEN
If all models in the chain are OPEN, the story is deferred with _failureReason: all_models_unavailable and the next story is attempted
Each fallback attempt emits a model_fallback event to spiral_events.jsonl with primary model, fallback model, and reason
A bats test mocks all models as OPEN and verifies the story receives _failureReason: all_models_unavailable without infinite looping
technicalNotes: Depends on the circuit breaker implementation (story: Add circuit breaker for LLM API calls with half-open recovery)
Model IDs must map to the correct --model flag for each CLI tool (claude: claude-sonnet-4-5, amp: same, codex: gpt-4o)
Cost tracking must account for per-model pricing when a fallback is used
The fallback chain applies at the story-attempt level — a story started on sonnet completes on sonnet even if haiku is cheaper

userStories.title: Enforce shfmt formatting on all spiral bash scripts in CI
userStories.description: Add shfmt-based formatting checks for all .sh files in the SPIRAL repository. This enforces consistent style (2-space indentation, Google shell style) across spiral.sh, ralph.sh, and all lib/ helpers. A CI step and optional pre-commit hook block merges if any script is not properly formatted.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `shfmt -d -i 2 -ci spiral.sh ralph/ralph.sh lib/*.sh` exits 0 when all files are correctly formatted
GitHub Actions workflow step shfmt-check runs on every PR and fails if formatting diff is non-empty
A pre-commit hook config entry (.pre-commit-config.yaml) is added using mvdan/sh shfmt hook
setup.sh includes a check that shfmt is installed and prints install instructions if missing
All existing .sh files are formatted to pass the check before this story is marked complete
technicalNotes: Use shfmt -i 2 -ci (2-space indent, case-indent) matching Google Shell Style Guide
shfmt can be installed via `brew install shfmt`, `go install mvdan.cc/sh/v3/cmd/shfmt@latest`, or downloaded as a binary
Add shfmt to the --doctor dependency check in spiral_doctor.sh
Do not apply shfmt to vendored or generated files

userStories.title: Inject trace_id and span_id into every spiral_events.jsonl entry
userStories.description: Correlate all JSONL log entries with the active OpenTelemetry trace by injecting trace_id and span_id fields derived from the W3C traceparent environment variable. This allows a log aggregator to join spiral_events.jsonl lines with OTLP trace spans without a separate log-trace correlation service.
acceptanceCriteria: log_ralph_event() extracts trace_id and span_id from TRACEPARENT env var (format: 00-<trace_id>-<span_id>-<flags>) if set
All emitted JSONL lines include `"trace_id":"<32hex>"` and `"span_id":"<16hex>"` fields when TRACEPARENT is set
When TRACEPARENT is not set, the fields are omitted (no null values)
A bats test sets TRACEPARENT=00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01 and verifies the extracted fields in the emitted log line
SPIRAL_RUN_ID is also verified to appear in every log line as `"run_id"` (completeness check)
technicalNotes: Parse traceparent with bash parameter expansion: trace_id=${TRACEPARENT:3:32}, span_id=${TRACEPARENT:36:16}
This extends the existing W3C traceparent propagation story by ensuring the IDs flow into JSONL lines, not just outbound env vars
The correlation enables `jq 'select(.trace_id=="<id>")' spiral_events.jsonl` to reconstruct a full story timeline from logs

userStories.title: Harden GitHub Actions workflow with SHA-pinned actions and least-privilege permissions
userStories.description: Harden the SPIRAL CI workflow for self-hosted runners by pinning all action versions to full SHA digests, adding step-security/harden-runner for egress auditing, and using minimal job-level permissions. This prevents supply-chain attacks via compromised upstream actions.
acceptanceCriteria: All GitHub Actions workflow steps using external actions are pinned to a full SHA commit digest (e.g., `actions/checkout@<sha>`)
step-security/harden-runner@<sha> is added as the first step in all jobs with `egress-policy: audit`
All jobs declare `permissions: contents: read` at the job level and only elevate permissions where explicitly required
No workflow uses `pull_request_target` with untrusted code checkout (documented as forbidden in workflow comments)
A comment documents that `egress-policy: block` should be enabled after an audit period
technicalNotes: SHA-pinning prevents a compromised upstream action from injecting malicious code — use Dependabot to keep SHAs current
Recommend ephemeral self-hosted runners (--ephemeral flag) over persistent runners in .github/SECURITY.md
OIDC-based credentials (role-to-assume) eliminate the need for stored long-lived secrets
Document in .github/SECURITY.md that self-hosted runners should only be used for private repos

userStories.title: Add named exit code constants to spiral.sh for programmatic error handling
userStories.description: Replace ad-hoc exit 1 calls in spiral.sh with named, documented exit code constants. Define readonly constants at the top of the script so that CI pipelines and the SPIRAL_ON_COMPLETE hook can distinguish between failure modes programmatically.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 8 named exit code constants are defined as `readonly ERR_<NAME>=N` at the top of spiral.sh, with N in range 2-125
All bare `exit 1` calls in spiral.sh are replaced with `exit $ERR_<RELEVANT_CODE>`
A reference table of exit codes and their meanings is maintained in a comment block near the constants
The spiral_doctor.sh --help output lists the exit code table
A bats test verifies that triggering each distinct error condition produces the documented exit code
technicalNotes: Reserve exit code 0 for full success, 1 for unclassified errors (never intentionally used), 2 for misuse (wrong args)
Exit codes 3-125 are safe for scripts; 126/127 are reserved by the shell; 128+ indicate signal deaths
Consistent exit codes allow `$?` checks in the SPIRAL_ON_COMPLETE hook to trigger different downstream actions

userStories.title: Use pnpm global virtual store for node_modules deduplication across worktrees
userStories.description: When pnpm is detected as the package manager, configure new worktrees to share pnpm's global virtual store rather than copying node_modules. This reduces per-worktree disk usage by 50-80% for Node.js projects with large dependency trees.
acceptanceCriteria: run_parallel_ralph.sh detects pnpm by checking `command -v pnpm` before creating worktrees
When pnpm is available, each worktree is initialised with `cd <worktree> && pnpm install --prefer-offline` instead of copying node_modules
A pnpm-workspace.yaml symlink or copy is created in each worktree if it exists in the repo root
A SPIRAL_SKIP_PNPM_DEDUP=1 env var disables this behaviour as an escape hatch
If pnpm install fails in a worktree, fall back to copying node_modules from the main worktree with a warning
technicalNotes: pnpm's global virtual store location is `pnpm store path`; symlinks from node_modules/.pnpm point into this store
Only activate this for worktrees created during parallel runs; single-worker runs are unaffected
This does NOT require sparse-checkout — it only changes how node_modules are populated

userStories.title: Stream-process large prd.json with jq --stream to avoid full in-memory parse
userStories.description: For prd.json files exceeding SPIRAL_PRD_STREAM_THRESHOLD_KB (default: 512KB), use `jq --stream` to extract pending story IDs without loading the entire document into memory. This supports PRDs with hundreds of stories or large embedded specs without hitting jq's memory limits.
acceptanceCriteria: A helper function get_pending_story_ids() in ralph.sh detects prd.json file size and switches to `jq --stream` when it exceeds SPIRAL_PRD_STREAM_THRESHOLD_KB
The streaming version produces identical output to the non-streaming version on the same input (verified by a bats test with a 1000-story synthetic prd.json)
SPIRAL_PRD_STREAM_THRESHOLD_KB is documented in spiral.config.example.sh
The non-streaming path (small files) is unchanged and no performance regression is introduced for typical prd.json files
A bats test exercises both code paths and compares their output
technicalNotes: jq --stream emits [path, value] pairs; use `tostream` / `fromstream` patterns to reconstruct objects
Streaming is only needed for the story selection query, not for the passes=true update (which uses atomic write-and-replace already)
jq 1.6+ is required for --stream support — document minimum jq version in spiral_doctor.sh

userStories.title: Add shellcheck static analysis gate to CI for all bash scripts
userStories.description: ShellCheck detects semantic errors in bash scripts (unquoted variables, incorrect conditionals, subshell bugs) that shfmt cannot — shfmt only enforces formatting. Without ShellCheck, subtle runtime bugs in spiral.sh, ralph.sh, and lib/ scripts slip through. CI must enforce zero ShellCheck violations at severity=error.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs `shellcheck --severity=error` over all `.sh` files in the repo and fails on any violation
ShellCheck version is pinned in a requirements/CI config file to prevent surprise failures from upstream changes
A pre-commit hook stage runs shellcheck on modified `.sh` files before commit
`spiral-doctor` verifies shellcheck is installed and reports its version
technicalNotes: ShellCheck is distinct from shfmt — shfmt is style/formatting, shellcheck is semantic correctness
Use `koalaman/shellcheck-action` GitHub Action with explicit `version:` pin (e.g., v0.10.0)
Output format: `--format=gcc` for GitHub Actions annotation integration
Pair with pre-commit hook using `repo: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck-precommit`

userStories.title: Add mypy strict type checking enforcement to Python CI pipeline
userStories.description: SPIRAL's Python scripts (spiral_dashboard.py, route_stories.py, check_done.py, etc.) currently lack static type checking enforcement in CI. mypy in strict mode catches type errors, missing annotations, and incorrect return types before runtime. This prevents a class of production bugs and makes refactoring safer.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs `mypy --strict` over all Python source files and fails on any type error
mypy version is pinned in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.mypy]`
A `pyproject.toml` section configures mypy with `python_version`, `strict = true`, and per-module overrides for gradual adoption
CI job reports mypy output as annotations on PRs using `--output json` piped to a formatter
technicalNotes: Use `dmypy` daemon for faster incremental checks during local development
For gradual adoption start with `check_untyped_defs = true` then escalate to `disallow_untyped_defs = true`
Pin version: `mypy==1.11` in dev dependencies to avoid CI flakiness on upstream releases
Reference: https://mypy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/existing_code.html for phased rollout strategy

userStories.title: Generate CHANGELOG.md automatically using git-cliff on tagged releases
userStories.description: SPIRAL commits follow conventional-commit style but has no automated CHANGELOG generation. git-cliff parses commit history using a regex-based config, groups by type (feat, fix, refactor), and outputs a formatted CHANGELOG.md. This makes releases traceable and documents what changed between iterations without manual effort.
acceptanceCriteria: `cliff.toml` config exists at repo root specifying commit parsing patterns and CHANGELOG template
Running `git-cliff --output CHANGELOG.md` from repo root produces a valid, correctly grouped CHANGELOG
A `make changelog` or `./spiral.sh --changelog` target generates the CHANGELOG for the current HEAD
CI validates that `CHANGELOG.md` is updated on release tags via git-cliff --unreleased check
CHANGELOG groups entries by: feat, fix, perf, refactor, docs, test, ci
technicalNotes: Use git-cliff (Rust binary, zero Node.js dependency): https://git-cliff.org/
Config: `cliff.toml` with `[git] conventional_commits = true` and `[changelog] header/body/footer` templates
Install via `cargo install git-cliff` or download binary from GitHub releases
GitHub Actions: add `git-cliff-action` step on `push: tags: ['v*']` to auto-update CHANGELOG and commit

userStories.title: Add pytest code coverage reporting with configurable fail-under threshold
userStories.description: SPIRAL's Python unit tests run in CI but have no coverage measurement or enforcement gate. Without a fail-under threshold, coverage can silently regress. pytest-cov generates HTML/XML/terminal reports and enforces a minimum coverage percentage, ensuring new code is tested before merging.
acceptanceCriteria: CI runs `pytest --cov=spiral --cov-report=xml:coverage.xml --cov-report=html:htmlcov --cov-fail-under=80`
HTML coverage report is uploaded as a GitHub Actions artifact on each CI run
Coverage summary is appended to GitHub Actions step summary via `--cov-report markdown-append:$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`
Coverage threshold is stored in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.coverage.report] fail_under = 80`
CI fails if overall coverage drops below the configured threshold
technicalNotes: Install: `uv add --dev pytest-cov coverage[toml]`
pyproject.toml config: `[tool.coverage.run] source = ["spiral"]` and `[tool.coverage.report] show_missing = true`
For trend tracking upload `coverage.xml` to Codecov: `codecov/codecov-action@v4`
Reference: https://pytest-cov.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reporting.html

userStories.title: Add stale story detection flagging stories inactive beyond SPIRAL_STALE_DAYS
userStories.description: Stories that remain in `todo` or `in_progress` status for extended periods without retry attempts become stale and indicate blocked or forgotten work. A configurable staleness detector warns operators and optionally iceboxes stories beyond a threshold, keeping the active backlog honest and actionable.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_STALE_DAYS env var (default: 7) controls the staleness threshold for active runs
At loop startup SPIRAL logs a warning for each story where `last_attempted` timestamp exceeds SPIRAL_STALE_DAYS
The spiral_dashboard.py overview section highlights stale stories with a distinct visual indicator
A `--stale-report` flag to spiral.sh prints all stale stories with their ages and last attempt timestamps
Stale stories are included in spiral_events.jsonl with event type `story_stale_detected`
technicalNotes: Add `last_attempted` ISO 8601 timestamp field to each prd.json story when ralph.sh begins working on it
Python: `datetime.fromisoformat(story['last_attempted'])` compared to `datetime.now() - timedelta(days=SPIRAL_STALE_DAYS)`
Dashboard: use CSS class `stale-story` with red/orange badge in the story table
Reference: https://resources.scrumalliance.org/Article/scrum-stale for threshold guidance

userStories.title: Add SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL env var for configurable output verbosity
userStories.description: spiral.sh currently emits all diagnostic output regardless of context. In production unattended runs, verbose debug output creates noise and obscures real errors. SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL (DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR) allows operators to control verbosity: DEBUG for development, WARN/ERROR for CI, reducing log volume and improving signal-to-noise ratio.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL accepts values: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR (case-insensitive, default: INFO)
All spiral.sh output is routed through a `log_msg LEVEL message` shell function that filters by SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL
DEBUG messages include function name and line number context for traceability
SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL is recorded in .spiral/_checkpoint.json for debugging reproducibility on resume
spiral_events.jsonl entries include a `level` field matching the SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL scale
technicalNotes: Shell log function pattern: `log_msg() { local lvl=$1; shift; [[ ${LOG_LEVELS[$lvl]} -ge ${LOG_LEVELS[$SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL]} ]] && echo "[$lvl] $*" >&2; }`
Use associative array for level ordering: `declare -A LOG_LEVELS=([DEBUG]=0 [INFO]=1 [WARN]=2 [ERROR]=3)`
Requires bash 4.0+ for associative arrays (already assumed by existing spiral.sh)
Structured log entries: `{ "ts": "...", "level": "INFO", "msg": "...", "phase": "I" }`

userStories.title: Run Phase V validation only on files touched by current story (incremental validation)
userStories.description: Phase V currently runs the full SPIRAL_VALIDATE_CMD test suite on every story attempt, even when the story only modifies 2-3 files. Incremental validation runs only the tests covering changed files (via pytest --testpaths or vitest --related), reducing Phase V time by 60-90% for large projects and making the loop significantly faster.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh reads the `filesTouch` field from prd.json for the current story to determine changed files
When SPIRAL_INCREMENTAL_VALIDATE=true ralph.sh constructs a targeted test command covering only those files
For pytest projects: tests are filtered to modules derived from filesTouch entries
For vitest projects: `vitest run --related <filesTouch entries>` is used
SPIRAL_INCREMENTAL_VALIDATE defaults to false to preserve existing behavior; full suite still runs at the final iteration gate
technicalNotes: Differential analysis: feed `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD` and filesTouch to determine scope
Pytest path mapping: `src/foo/bar.py` to test file `tests/test_bar.py` via configurable `SPIRAL_TEST_PREFIX`
Vitest `--related` flag natively handles this: `vitest run --related src/foo/bar.ts`
Reference: https://sdtimes.com/devops/demystifying-differential-and-incremental-analysis-for-static-code-analysis-within-devops/

userStories.title: Add SPIRAL_PRE_PHASE_HOOK and SPIRAL_POST_PHASE_HOOK for user-defined phase scripts
userStories.description: Different projects need custom actions at specific SPIRAL phases — e.g., seeding a test database before Phase V, or posting a Slack notification after Phase I. Generic pre/post phase hooks (env vars pointing to executable scripts) enable this without modifying spiral.sh itself, following the Unix composability philosophy.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_PRE_PHASE_HOOK and SPIRAL_POST_PHASE_HOOK accept a path to an executable script
The hook script receives SPIRAL_CURRENT_PHASE (I/R/M/V/G) and SPIRAL_CURRENT_STORY_ID as env vars
spiral.sh sources the hook before/after each phase transition and captures its exit code
A non-zero exit code from a pre-phase hook aborts the current story attempt and records the failure reason
Hook execution is logged to spiral_events.jsonl with event type `phase_hook_pre` / `phase_hook_post` and duration
technicalNotes: Pattern: `if [[ -x "${SPIRAL_PRE_PHASE_HOOK:-}" ]]; then "$SPIRAL_PRE_PHASE_HOOK" || handle_hook_failure; fi`
Pass story context via env: `SPIRAL_CURRENT_PHASE SPIRAL_CURRENT_STORY_ID SPIRAL_RUN_ID SPIRAL_ITERATION`
Timeout hook execution with `timeout ${SPIRAL_HOOK_TIMEOUT:-30}` to prevent hooks from stalling the loop
Document hook contract in SPIRAL README: expected env vars, exit code semantics, and example scripts

userStories.title: Add SPIRAL_WORKSPACE_CLEANUP to auto-prune transient artifacts on successful run completion
userStories.description: Completed SPIRAL runs accumulate research cache files, intermediate JSON files, old iteration summaries, and stale log entries in `.spiral/`. Without cleanup, these consume disk space over many runs. SPIRAL_WORKSPACE_CLEANUP=true triggers a post-completion sweep that archives completed run artifacts and removes transient files.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_WORKSPACE_CLEANUP env var (default: false) enables post-run cleanup
When enabled cleanup runs only after 100% of stories reach `passed` or `skipped` status
Cleanup removes: `.spiral/research_cache/` entries older than SPIRAL_CACHE_TTL, iteration summary JSONs older than 5 iterations, and zero-byte log files
Cleanup preserves: `prd.json`, `spiral_events.jsonl`, `_checkpoint.json`, and `results.tsv`
Cleanup actions are logged to spiral_events.jsonl with event type `workspace_cleanup` and bytes_freed metric
technicalNotes: Implement as a `cleanup_workspace()` bash function called at the end of spiral.sh main loop
Use `find .spiral/research_cache -mtime +${SPIRAL_CACHE_TTL:-7} -delete` for expired cache entries
Archive old iteration summaries: `tar -czf .spiral/archive/iter_summaries_$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz .spiral/_iteration_summary_*.json`
Report bytes freed: `du -sb .spiral/` before and after and emit delta to spiral_events.jsonl

userStories.title: Create annotated git tag on successful SPIRAL run completion with run metadata
userStories.description: When all stories in a SPIRAL run pass, the resulting codebase state has no persistent git marker. Creating an annotated git tag (e.g., `spiral/run-42-complete`) with run metadata (stories completed, iteration count, duration) makes it trivially easy to identify and checkout the state produced by any completed SPIRAL run.
acceptanceCriteria: When all stories reach `passed` status spiral.sh creates an annotated git tag `spiral/run-{SPIRAL_RUN_ID}-complete`
The tag annotation includes: total stories completed, iterations taken, wall-clock duration, and final commit SHA
Tag creation is conditional on SPIRAL_CREATE_TAGS=true (default: false) to avoid cluttering repos
Tag creation is logged to spiral_events.jsonl with event type `run_complete_tagged`
If the tag already exists it is updated with --force only when SPIRAL_CREATE_TAGS=true
technicalNotes: Shell: `git tag -a "spiral/run-${SPIRAL_RUN_ID}-complete" -m "SPIRAL run complete: ${story_count} stories in ${iteration_count} iterations"`
Include ISO 8601 timestamp in tag annotation for traceability
Push tag if SPIRAL_AUTO_PUSH_TAGS=true: `git push origin "spiral/run-${SPIRAL_RUN_ID}-complete"`
Reference: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-tag for annotated tag format

userStories.title: Pinchtab Phase V E2E browser assertions integration
userStories.description: Add SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_URL config var and pinchtab E2E block to Phase V of spiral.sh. When set, Phase V calls pinchtab CLI after pytest to navigate to SPIRAL_DEV_URL and extract page text as a lightweight browser assertion. Text mode costs ~800 tokens vs ~10,000 for a screenshot. A custom SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_E2E_CMD override supports login flows and multi-step assertions.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_URL is documented in spiral.config.sh (commented out) and templates/spiral.config.example.sh
When SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_URL is unset, Phase V behaves exactly as before (no regression)
When SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_URL and SPIRAL_DEV_URL are set and pinchtab CLI is present, Phase V runs nav + text steps and prints first 20 lines of page text
When SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_E2E_CMD is set, Phase V runs that command instead of the default nav+text steps
A pinchtab failure prints a WARNING but does not change the Phase V validation result (VALIDATE_EXIT is unchanged)
uv run pytest passes
technicalNotes: Pinchtab block goes in spiral.sh after the Chrome DevTools screenshot block (before write_checkpoint V)
Use: pinchtab --server "$SPIRAL_PINCHTAB_URL" nav / text
Guard with: command -v pinchtab to print a helpful install hint if missing
ralph/CLAUDE.md already updated: clarifies Chrome DevTools MCP is for Phase I inline checks; pinchtab is Phase V only
Do NOT start/stop pinchtab server from spiral.sh — user manages the server lifecycle externally

userStories.title: Enable Anthropic prompt caching for ralph.sh system prompts
userStories.description: Use Anthropic's prompt caching beta (cache_control breakpoints) to cache the large system prompt sent to Claude on every ralph.sh invocation. Cache hits reduce input token cost by 90% and latency by 85%, compounding significantly across hundreds of story attempts per run.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh adds cache_control: {type: ephemeral} breakpoint after the system prompt in API payloads
cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens fields are extracted from API responses and appended to the structured log
results.tsv gains cache_hit (boolean) and cache_read_tokens columns
Integration test verifies that on second invocation with identical system prompt, cache_read_input_tokens > 0
technicalNotes: Requires X-Anthropic-Beta: prompt-caching-2024-07-31 header on API requests
Cache TTL is 5 minutes for ephemeral; keyed on exact prefix byte sequence
Minimum cacheable block is 1024 tokens; system prompts in ralph.sh comfortably exceed this
Official docs: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-caching

userStories.title: Add gitleaks secret scanning gate before AI-generated code is committed
userStories.description: Run gitleaks detect on the staged diff before committing AI-generated code to prevent accidentally committing API keys, tokens, or credentials that an LLM might hallucinate into implementation files. This is a critical security gate unique to AI-assisted development workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh runs `gitleaks detect --staged --report-format json` before git commit in Phase I
If gitleaks finds any findings, the commit is aborted and the story is marked _failureReason: 'secret_detected'
SPIRAL_SKIP_SECRET_SCAN=true bypasses the gate with a structured warning log entry
spiral-doctor verifies gitleaks binary is present and reports its version
technicalNotes: gitleaks GitHub: https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks
Command: gitleaks detect --source . --staged --report-format json --report-path .spiral/gitleaks-report.json
Allow-list spiral's own config variable names via .gitleaks.toml to prevent false positives
Alternative: trufflehog OSS (https://github.com/trufflesecurity/trufflehog)

userStories.title: Truncate story context to fit Claude context window using token counting
userStories.description: Before sending a story implementation request to Claude, measure the assembled prompt token count using the Anthropic token-counting API (or tiktoken approximation). If the count exceeds a configurable threshold (default: 180,000 tokens), truncate the least-relevant context sections to fit. Prevents silent context truncation by the API that causes incomplete implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh calls lib/truncate_context.py to measure token count before every API call
Truncation priority: drop _researchOutput first, then hints, then filesTouch context; never drop story spec
When truncation occurs, a structured warning is emitted with original_tokens and truncated_tokens fields
SPIRAL_CONTEXT_LIMIT env var overrides the default 180000 token threshold
Unit tests cover: truncation ordering, edge case where story spec alone exceeds limit, no-op when under threshold
technicalNotes: Anthropic token-counting endpoint: POST /v1/messages/count_tokens (beta, no charge)
tiktoken cl100k_base is a reasonable local approximation: https://github.com/openai/tiktoken
Official token counting docs: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/token-counting
Cache token counts per (story_id, attempt) to avoid redundant API calls on retries

userStories.title: Add `spiral.sh --rollback STORY_ID` to revert a passed story's commit
userStories.description: Provide a one-command way to revert the git commit(s) associated with a specific passed story, resetting its prd.json status back to pending. This complements the existing --replay flag and closes the full undo/redo cycle for story implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: `spiral.sh --rollback US-042` reads _passedCommit SHA from prd.json and runs `git revert --no-edit <sha>`
prd.json story status resets to pending; _passedCommit, _passedAt, and _adrPath fields are cleared
If _passedCommit is absent, print actionable error message and exit 1
Rollback is rejected with an error if there are uncommitted working-tree changes
Unit test covers: successful rollback, missing _passedCommit error, dirty working-tree guard
technicalNotes: `git revert --no-edit <sha>` creates a new revert commit without opening an editor
Prefer revert over reset --hard to preserve linear history and auditability
Pair with --replay to form a full redo cycle: rollback then replay
git revert docs: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-revert
dependencies: Record _passedCommit git SHA in prd.json when story passes

userStories.title: Create GitHub PR per passed story when SPIRAL_CREATE_PRS=true
userStories.description: After a story passes validation, optionally push the story commit to a feature branch and open a GitHub Pull Request using the gh CLI. Enables human review of AI-generated code before it reaches main, supporting team workflows where all changes go through PR review.
acceptanceCriteria: When SPIRAL_CREATE_PRS=true, ralph.sh pushes story commit to branch `spiral/<STORY_ID>` and calls `gh pr create`
PR title matches the story title; PR body includes story description, acceptanceCriteria, and SPIRAL_RUN_ID
PR URL is stored in prd.json as _prUrl field on the story
If gh CLI is not authenticated, emit actionable error and skip PR creation without failing the story
SPIRAL_PR_BASE_BRANCH env var sets target base branch (default: main)
technicalNotes: GitHub CLI pr create docs: https://cli.github.com/manual/gh_pr_create
Command: gh pr create --base main --head spiral/US-042 --title '...' --body '...' --label 'spiral-ai'
Auto-create 'spiral-ai' label if it does not exist via `gh label create`
SPIRAL_PR_DRAFT=true creates draft PRs to prevent auto-merge triggers

userStories.title: Add Ollama local LLM fallback when Claude API is unreachable
userStories.description: Configure a fallback LLM provider (Ollama with qwen2.5-coder or codellama) that activates when Claude API returns repeated connection errors or 529 overload responses. Improves offline resilience and reduces hard dependency on a single API provider during extended outages.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_OLLAMA_FALLBACK_MODEL env var specifies Ollama model name (e.g., qwen2.5-coder:32b)
After 3 consecutive Claude API connection failures (curl exit 7 or 28), ralph.sh switches to Ollama for the current story
Ollama requests use the OpenAI-compatible endpoint at SPIRAL_OLLAMA_HOST (default: http://localhost:11434/v1)
Stories implemented via Ollama are tagged _model: 'ollama/<model>' in prd.json
spiral-doctor checks Ollama reachability when SPIRAL_OLLAMA_FALLBACK_MODEL is configured
technicalNotes: Ollama OpenAI-compatible API docs: https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/openai.md
Endpoint: POST http://localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions with standard OpenAI payload format
Recommended coding models: qwen2.5-coder:32b, codellama:34b, deepseek-coder-v2:16b
curl exit code 7 = connection refused; exit code 28 = timeout; both indicate API unreachable

userStories.title: Add LLM self-review gate (Phase I.5) between implementation and validation
userStories.description: After Phase I (implement) and before Phase V (validate), send the generated diff to Claude with a structured code-review prompt asking it to identify bugs, security issues, and spec deviations. If the self-review flags critical issues, re-enter Phase I with the issue list as additional context, reducing first-attempt test failures.
acceptanceCriteria: New Phase I.5 (REVIEW) sends story spec + git diff to Claude with a JSON-mode review prompt
Review response schema: {issues: [{severity: critical|major|minor, location: string, description: string}]}
If any issue has severity='critical', Phase I is re-entered with the issue list injected into the implementation prompt
SPIRAL_SKIP_SELF_REVIEW=true disables Phase I.5; review is also skipped on retries beyond SPIRAL_MAX_RETRIES/2
review_tokens column is added to results.tsv to track self-review cost
technicalNotes: Use haiku model for self-review to minimise cost; escalate to sonnet only on complex diffs
Limit git diff to 500 lines to prevent context overflow in the review step
Review system prompt should be a cacheable constant using prompt caching
Anthropic JSON mode reference: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/test-and-evaluate/strengthen-guardrails/increase-consistency

userStories.title: Generate Conventional Commit message from story metadata in ralph.sh
userStories.description: Replace the current freeform commit message with a Conventional Commits-compliant message derived from story metadata: type from story tags, scope from the primary filesTouch path, and subject from the story title. Enables automated CHANGELOG generation and semantic versioning tools to parse SPIRAL commit history.
acceptanceCriteria: Commit message format: `<type>(<scope>): <story title>` with Story and SPIRAL-Run trailers in the footer
Type is derived from story tags field (feat, fix, chore, refactor, test, docs); defaults to feat when absent
Scope is the top-level directory of the first filesTouch entry (e.g., lib, ralph, spiral)
Footer lines: `Story: <STORY_ID>` and `SPIRAL-Run: <SPIRAL_RUN_ID>`
Unit test verifies correct message format for stories with tags, without tags, and with no filesTouch entries
technicalNotes: Conventional Commits v1.0 spec: https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/
Git trailer format: key-colon-space-value lines after a blank line in the commit body
git-cliff uses these trailers for automatic CHANGELOG generation
Validate with: echo '<message>' | npx commitlint in CI

userStories.title: Add pre-flight API cost projection before spiral.sh loop start
userStories.description: Before starting a SPIRAL run, estimate the total API cost of implementing all pending stories using historical results.tsv token data and current model pricing. Display a cost projection table and prompt for confirmation when the projected cost exceeds SPIRAL_COST_WARN_THRESHOLD. Prevents surprise bills on large PRD files.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh prints a cost projection table (model tier, story count, estimated tokens, estimated USD) before entering the main loop
Projection uses mean tokens-per-story from recent results.tsv rows; falls back to SPIRAL_DEFAULT_TOKENS_PER_STORY (default: 8000) when insufficient history
When projected cost > SPIRAL_COST_WARN_THRESHOLD (default: $5.00), prompts `Estimated cost: $X.XX — Continue? [y/N]`
--yes flag skips the confirmation prompt for non-interactive CI environments
Projection is skipped when results.tsv has fewer than 5 data rows
technicalNotes: Claude model pricing page: https://www.anthropic.com/pricing
haiku-4-5: ~$0.80/MTok input; sonnet-4-6: ~$3.00/MTok input; opus-4-6: ~$15.00/MTok input
Implement projection in lib/cost_project.py; expose via main.py estimate subcommand
Show confidence range: projection ± 1 std deviation from results.tsv sample

userStories.title: Parallelize Phase V test execution with pytest-xdist or parallel bats
userStories.description: When SPIRAL_PARALLEL_TESTS=true, run the Phase V test suite in parallel across available CPU cores using pytest-xdist (Python) or GNU parallel (bats). Reduces Phase V wall-clock time on large test suites, directly improving story throughput on multi-core workers.
acceptanceCriteria: SPIRAL_PARALLEL_TESTS=true activates parallel test execution
SPIRAL_TEST_WORKERS controls parallelism level (default: nproc/2, minimum 1)
For pytest suites, SPIRAL_VALIDATE_CMD automatically gains `-n $SPIRAL_TEST_WORKERS` when xdist is installed
For bats suites, SPIRAL_VALIDATE_CMD gains `--jobs $SPIRAL_TEST_WORKERS` when GNU parallel is available
spiral-doctor reports whether pytest-xdist and GNU parallel are installed
technicalNotes: pytest-xdist docs: https://pytest-xdist.readthedocs.io/en/stable/
bats parallel support: https://bats-core.readthedocs.io/en/stable/parallel-support.html
Detect xdist: `python -c 'import xdist; print(xdist.__version__)'`
Detect GNU parallel: `command -v parallel && parallel --version`
Warn when SPIRAL_PARALLEL_TESTS=true but tests use shared file-system state

userStories.title: Emit SARIF 2.1.0 report from static analysis gates for GitHub Code Scanning
userStories.description: Output static analysis results (shellcheck, semgrep, pylint) in SARIF 2.1.0 format and upload to GitHub Advanced Security Code Scanning. Provides inline PR annotations for security and quality findings without requiring a separate plugin installation.
acceptanceCriteria: CI workflow runs `semgrep --sarif --output semgrep.sarif` and uploads via github/codeql-action/upload-sarif
shellcheck output is converted to SARIF using the shellcheck-sarif converter tool
SARIF files are also uploaded as GitHub Actions artifacts for offline inspection
Upload step uses `continue-on-error: true` so SARIF findings do not fail the build
SARIF output passes schema validation against https://json.schemastore.org/sarif-2.1.0.json
technicalNotes: SARIF 2.1.0 spec: https://docs.oasis-open.org/sarif/sarif/v2.1.0/sarif-v2.1.0.html
GitHub upload-sarif action: https://github.com/github/codeql-action/tree/main/upload-sarif
shellcheck-sarif converter: https://github.com/rust-phile/shellcheck-sarif (cargo install shellcheck-sarif)
Semgrep SARIF: `semgrep scan --sarif --output results.sarif`

userStories.title: Guard against story scope creep: warn when implementation touches > N files
userStories.description: After Phase I commits the story implementation, count the number of files modified. If the count exceeds SPIRAL_MAX_FILES_PER_STORY (default: 10), emit a structured scope-creep warning. Scope creep is a leading indicator that a story should be decomposed and correlates with higher first-attempt failure rates.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh counts modified files via `git diff --name-only HEAD~1 | wc -l` after Phase I commit
When count exceeds SPIRAL_MAX_FILES_PER_STORY, a scope_creep event is emitted to spiral_events.jsonl with the file list
SPIRAL_SCOPE_CREEP_ACTION=abort marks story _failureReason: 'scope_creep' and triggers retry with decomposition hint
SPIRAL_SCOPE_CREEP_ACTION=warn (default) logs the warning but continues to Phase V
spiral_dashboard.py highlights stories that triggered scope-creep warnings in the story table
technicalNotes: Exclude lock files and generated files from the count via a configurable .spiralignore pattern list
Example exclusions: *.lock, *_generated.*, *pb.go
Combine with auto-trigger decomposition story for a full guard pipeline
Google Engineering recommends <10 files per change for reviewability

userStories.title: Add API rate-limit budget forecasting widget to spiral_dashboard.py
userStories.description: Based on current story velocity and per-request token usage from results.tsv, forecast when the daily API rate limit will be exhausted and display a projected completion time. Helps users schedule runs, manage quota, and avoid unexpected mid-run throttling.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows: current burn rate (tokens/hour), projected daily-limit exhaustion time, and projected run completion time
Forecast uses a rolling 1-hour window of results.tsv timestamps and token usage columns
When projected exhaustion is less than 2 hours away, the widget is highlighted in amber
SPIRAL_DAILY_TOKEN_LIMIT env var sets the budget ceiling for forecast calculation (default: 1,000,000)
Forecast widget is hidden when results.tsv has fewer than 3 recent data rows
technicalNotes: Anthropic rate limits reference: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/api/rate-limits
Default tier: 40k tokens/min, 1M tokens/day (varies by usage tier)
Burn rate formula: sum(input_tokens + output_tokens for rows in last 3600s) / 3600
Display format: 'At current pace, daily limit reached in ~2h 15m (at 14:32)'

userStories.title: Validate prd.json for non-UTF-8 bytes and control characters at startup
userStories.description: Add a startup preflight check that validates prd.json is well-formed UTF-8 and contains no ASCII control characters (0x00-0x1F except tab and newline) in string values. LLM-generated content occasionally embeds null bytes or other control characters that silently corrupt downstream JSON parsing and bash variable assignments.
acceptanceCriteria: validate_preflight.sh checks prd.json UTF-8 validity using `python3 -c 'import json,sys; json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))'`
Additional scan checks all string values for control characters via regex matching bytes 0x00-0x08, 0x0b-0x0c, 0x0e-0x1f
On detection, emit structured error with field path and hex representation of the offending byte
SPIRAL_SANITIZE_PRD=true auto-strips control characters instead of aborting
Unit tests: valid file passes, file with null byte in description fails, SANITIZE mode strips and continues
technicalNotes: JSON UTF-8 requirement: RFC 8259 Section 8.1 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8259#section-8.1)
Shell-level check: `jq . prd.json > /dev/null 2>&1` exits non-zero on malformed input
Use json.loads() after reading raw bytes to obtain precise error offset
Common causes: LLM-generated content with Windows CRLF, smart quotes, or Word clipboard paste artifacts

userStories.title: Add `spiral status` subcommand with color-coded story progress table
userStories.description: Implement a spiral status CLI subcommand that prints a compact color-coded story progress table to stdout without launching the full dashboard server. Provides a fast at-a-glance view of story counts by status, current iteration, and estimated completion for use in terminal sessions and CI logs.
acceptanceCriteria: `spiral status` prints a table with columns: status, count, percentage, avg_retry_count
Header shows SPIRAL_RUN_ID and current iteration number
Color coding uses ANSI codes: green=passed, yellow=in_progress, red=skipped, grey=pending; respects NO_COLOR env var
`spiral status --json` outputs machine-readable JSON for CI pipeline consumption
Command completes in under 1 second (reads only prd.json and results.tsv, no API calls)
technicalNotes: Use rich library for table rendering: https://github.com/Textualize/rich
NO_COLOR standard: https://no-color.org/ — check `os.environ.get('NO_COLOR')` before applying ANSI
Falls back to plain aligned text when rich is not installed
Distinct from existing --status flag: this is a full subcommand with richer output and --json mode

userStories.title: Add `spiral search QUERY` subcommand to find stories by natural language
userStories.description: Implement a local semantic/fuzzy search over prd.json story titles, descriptions, and acceptanceCriteria, allowing users to quickly find a story ID without scrolling through the full PRD. Provides fast relevance-ranked results directly in the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: `spiral search 'rate limit retry'` prints top-5 matching stories with ID, title, status, and relevance score
Search covers title, description, and acceptanceCriteria fields
Uses rapidfuzz fuzzy matching when sentence-transformers is unavailable; uses cosine similarity when available
Results are printed as a compact table; --json flag outputs machine-readable JSON
Command exits 0 even when no results found; prints 'No matching stories found'
technicalNotes: rapidfuzz for fuzzy matching: https://github.com/rapidfuzz/RapidFuzz (pip install rapidfuzz)
sentence-transformers for semantic search: https://www.sbert.net/
Implement in lib/search_stories.py; expose via main.py search subcommand
Cache sentence-transformer embeddings in .spiral/story_embeddings.pkl to avoid recomputation

userStories.title: Auto-generate Architecture Decision Record (ADR) when story passes
userStories.description: After a story passes validation, generate a lightweight ADR markdown file capturing the design choices made during implementation. ADRs are placed in docs/decisions/ and committed as part of the story commit, building a long-term architectural knowledge base alongside the code.
acceptanceCriteria: On story pass, ralph.sh calls Claude (haiku) with the story spec + diff and an ADR generation prompt
Generated ADR follows MADR format: Title, Status: Accepted, Context, Decision, Consequences
ADR file is saved to docs/decisions/<STORY_ID>-<kebab-title>.md and included in the story commit
SPIRAL_SKIP_ADR=true disables ADR generation entirely
prd.json story record gains _adrPath field pointing to the generated ADR file
technicalNotes: MADR (Markdown Architectural Decision Records) format: https://adr.github.io/madr/
Use haiku model for ADR generation to minimise cost (~$0.001 per ADR)
Store the ADR prompt template in lib/prompts/adr_template.md for maintainability
adr-tools CLI (optional integration): https://github.com/npryce/adr-tools

userStories.title: Add `spiral compact-prd` command to strip transient runtime fields from prd.json
userStories.description: Provide a spiral compact-prd subcommand that strips transient runtime fields (_lastAttempt, _workerPid, _researchOutput, _routerScore) from completed/skipped stories in prd.json. Keeps the PRD file clean and human-readable as it accumulates hundreds of stories across multiple runs.
acceptanceCriteria: `spiral compact-prd` removes transient fields from all stories with status passed or skipped
Original prd.json is backed up to .spiral/prd_pre_compact_<timestamp>.json before modification
Write operation is atomic: write to prd.json.tmp then rename
Prints summary: 'Compacted N stories, removed M fields, saved X KB'
Unit test verifies that required schema fields (id, title, status) are never removed
technicalNotes: Fields to strip: _lastAttempt, _workerPid, _researchOutput, _routerScore, _lastResearchAttempt
Implement in lib/compact_prd.py; expose via main.py compact subcommand
Reuse the atomic write pattern from merge_stories.py
Add to spiral-doctor: warn when prd.json file size exceeds 1MB

userStories.title: Add SPIRAL_BRANCH_PREFIX for per-story feature branch creation and naming
userStories.description: Optionally check out a dedicated feature branch for each story before Phase I implementation, enabling clean PR-per-story workflows. Branch name follows `<SPIRAL_BRANCH_PREFIX>/<STORY_ID>` convention (e.g., spiral/US-042). After story passes, branch is merged back to the base branch or left open for PR creation.
acceptanceCriteria: When SPIRAL_BRANCH_PREFIX is set, ralph.sh creates branch `<SPIRAL_BRANCH_PREFIX>/<STORY_ID>` before Phase I
After Phase V passes, branch is merged to SPIRAL_BASE_BRANCH with --no-ff unless SPIRAL_CREATE_PRS=true
When SPIRAL_CREATE_PRS=true, branch is pushed to remote and left open for PR creation step
Branch creation is idempotent: reuses existing branch for the same STORY_ID to support replays
Branch is deleted after successful merge unless SPIRAL_KEEP_STORY_BRANCHES=true
technicalNotes: Use git worktree for parallel worker isolation rather than local branch switching
For sequential mode, use `git checkout -B spiral/US-042` (force create or reset)
Guard against 'branch already checked out in another worktree' with `git worktree list`
Git branching workflows: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Branching-Branching-Workflows

userStories.title: Add per-worker CPU and memory resource accounting columns to results.tsv
userStories.description: Record per-story CPU time (user+system seconds) and peak RSS memory alongside token counts in results.tsv. Enables identifying computationally expensive stories, correlating resource usage with model choice and story complexity, and feeding data into OOM watchdog threshold calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh wraps the Phase I+V execution block with `/usr/bin/time -f '%e %U %S %M'` measurement
results.tsv gains columns: wall_seconds, user_cpu_s, sys_cpu_s, peak_rss_kb
spiral_dashboard.py displays median and p95 resource usage grouped by model tier
When peak_rss_kb exceeds SPIRAL_WORKER_MEMORY_LIMIT, the existing OOM guard is triggered
Unit test verifies all four new columns are present in the results.tsv header and populated per row
technicalNotes: GNU time format string: '%e %U %S %M' (wall seconds, user CPU, sys CPU, max RSS KB)
macOS uses `/usr/bin/time -l` instead; detect platform with `uname -s`
Portable alternative: `{ TIMEFORMAT='%R %U %S'; time cmd; } 2>&1` in bash
GNU time man page: https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man1/time.1.html

userStories.title: Cache Gemini pre-analysis results per story ID to skip redundant calls on retry
userStories.description: On story retry (attempt 2 or 3), ralph.sh re-runs the Gemini pre-analysis for the same story, burning quota and adding 10-30 seconds. Since the story definition has not changed, the output is identical. Cache the Gemini result in .spiral/gemini-cache/{story_id}.json keyed by SPIRAL_RUN_ID and reuse it on retry.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh writes Gemini output to .spiral/gemini-cache/{story_id}.json after a successful call
On retry for the same story_id within the same SPIRAL_RUN_ID, the cache is used
Cache miss triggers a fresh Gemini call
Cache hits are logged as '[precontext] Gemini cache hit for {story_id}'
Cache directory is cleaned up at the start of each new Spiral run
technicalNotes: Store cache as JSON: {run_id, story_id, timestamp, content}
Validate: jq -r .run_id cache.json == $SPIRAL_RUN_ID
Add .spiral/gemini-cache/ to .gitignore

userStories.title: Add SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS to reuse Phase R output across iterations
userStories.description: Phase R runs a full web research pass every iteration even when the project has not changed. Add TTL-based caching: if _research_output.json is younger than SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS (default: 0 = disabled), skip Phase R and reuse the cached output. Useful for short-interval re-runs after manual story edits.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh checks _research_output.json mtime against SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS before Phase R
If file is newer than TTL: Phase R is skipped with '[Phase R] cache hit (Nh old)'
If SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS=0 (default): cache disabled, Phase R always runs
--skip-research flag still works as before
SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS documented in spiral.config.example.sh
technicalNotes: Use stat or python -c 'import os; print(os.path.getmtime(...))' for mtime
Compare: (now - mtime) / 3600 < SPIRAL_RESEARCH_CACHE_TTL_HOURS
Log cache age when reusing: '[Phase R] skipping -- research is 2h old (TTL: 6h)'

userStories.title: Fast-path small stories: skip Gemini pre-analysis for estimatedComplexity=small
userStories.description: The Gemini pre-analysis adds 10-30 seconds to every story including trivially small ones. For stories with estimatedComplexity=small and at most 2 filesTouch entries, skip the Gemini call entirely and go straight to Claude implementation. Saves 10-30 seconds per small story with no quality loss.
acceptanceCriteria: ralph.sh skips Gemini when story.estimatedComplexity == 'small' AND len(filesTouch) <= 2
Skip is logged: '[precontext] skipped -- small story with <= 2 file hints'
SPIRAL_GEMINI_SKIP_SMALL=false disables the fast-path (default: true)
Fast-path does not apply when SPIRAL_GEMINI_ANNOTATE_PROMPT is set
A bats test confirms the Gemini call is not made for qualifying stories
technicalNotes: Add the condition in ralph.sh before the Gemini invocation block
filesTouch length: jq '(.filesTouch // []) | length'

userStories.title: Compress old .spiral/ iteration artifacts with gzip to reduce disk usage
userStories.description: Each Spiral iteration writes several JSON files to .spiral/. On long runs these accumulate to tens of MB. Compress artifacts from iterations N-2 and older with gzip, keeping only the last 2 iterations uncompressed for easy inspection.
acceptanceCriteria: At the start of each iteration, JSON files from iter N-2 and older are gzip-compressed
Compressed files are named <original>.gz and originals are removed
Checkpoint resume logic handles a mix of compressed and uncompressed artifacts
Total .spiral/ disk usage is logged when SPIRAL_LOG_LEVEL=debug
A bats test confirms compression happens and originals are removed
technicalNotes: Use gzip without --keep to replace in place, or gzip --keep then rm
Skip compression if gzip is unavailable (log warning, continue)
Do not compress _checkpoint.json or latest-review.html (needed at runtime)

userStories.title: Implement Phase S (Story Validate) — auto-reject out-of-scope research stories
userStories.description: The Phase S stub exists in lib/phases/phase_s_story_validate.sh but has no implementation. Wire it into spiral.sh between Phase T and Phase M. Phase S reads candidate stories, checks each against prd.json goals[] and the constitution (if set), drops stories with no clear goal connection, and writes accepted stories to _validated_stories.json for Phase M.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh calls run_phase_story_validate() between Phase T and Phase M
Phase S produces .spiral/_validated_stories.json with only accepted stories
Stories with no connection to any prd.json goal[] are dropped with reason logged
Stories violating the constitution are also dropped when SPIRAL_SPECKIT_CONSTITUTION is set
Phase M reads _validated_stories.json instead of raw research output
A bats test with known off-topic stories confirms they are rejected
technicalNotes: Use Claude haiku as the reviewer with a simple YES/NO prompt against each goal
Fallback to keyword overlap check if the Claude call fails
Log acceptance rate per iteration to spiral_events.jsonl

userStories.title: Implement Phase 0 (Clarify) — interactive startup with time-limit and focus setting
userStories.description: The Phase 0 stub exists in lib/phases/phase_0_clarify.sh but has no implementation. Wire it into spiral.sh as a one-time pre-loop step. Phase 0 confirms focus area, asks how many hours to run (sets TIME_LIMIT_MINS), and optionally accepts initial story seeds from the user. Fully skipped in --gate proceed/skip mode.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh calls run_phase_clarify() once before the main loop, not on resume
Phase 0 asks 'How many hours should Spiral run? (Enter for unlimited)' and sets TIME_LIMIT_MINS
Phase 0 asks 'Any focus area for this session?' and sets SPIRAL_FOCUS if answered
Phase 0 accepts initial story seeds (one per line) and adds them to prd.json
With --gate proceed or --gate skip: Phase 0 is skipped entirely
Checkpoint records phase_0_complete: true so resume skips it
technicalNotes: Use bash read -t 60 -p '...' to avoid hanging on no input
Story seeds: convert plain text lines to minimal story objects with priority:medium
Output written to .spiral/_clarify_output.json for auditability

userStories.title: Atomic prd.json write in merge_stories.py using temp-file rename
userStories.description: merge_stories.py writes directly to prd.json in-place. A crash or SIGKILL mid-write produces a truncated or corrupt prd.json. Rewrite the write path to write to prd.json.tmp first, then atomically rename via os.replace(). This prevents the file from ever being caught in a half-written state.
acceptanceCriteria: merge_stories.py writes output to prd.json.tmp then os.replace() to prd.json
prd.json.tmp is removed on both success and exception via try/finally
Simulating a kill mid-write leaves prd.json intact from before the merge
A unit test mocks os.replace to raise and confirms prd.json is unchanged
Existing merge_stories.py unit tests continue to pass
technicalNotes: os.replace() is atomic on POSIX; on Windows it is safer than direct overwrite
Apply the same pattern to every other location that writes prd.json directly

userStories.title: Force-remove orphaned worker git worktree and branch on implementation timeout
userStories.description: When a parallel worker hits SPIRAL_IMPL_TIMEOUT, run_parallel_ralph.sh kills the process but leaves the git worktree and branch in place. These ghost worktrees consume disk, cause branch-already-checked-out errors on the next run, and bloat git internals. Add a post-kill cleanup step that force-removes the worktree and deletes the branch.
acceptanceCriteria: When a worker is killed by timeout, its worktree is removed within 10 seconds
The worker feature branch is deleted after worktree removal
If removal fails, a warning is logged but the run continues
spiral.sh --status reports no orphaned worktrees after a timeout scenario
Integration test with a mock hanging worker confirms cleanup fires
technicalNotes: Add cleanup_worker() called from the timeout handler in run_parallel_ralph.sh
Use: git worktree remove --force <path> && git branch -D <branch>
On Windows add a short sleep before removal to allow file handle release

userStories.title: Detect and stash dirty working tree before Phase I to prevent git errors
userStories.description: If a user edits files while Spiral is paused at Phase 0 or Gate, the working tree becomes dirty. Phase I then fails with confusing git errors when creating worktrees. Add a pre-Phase-I check: detect uncommitted changes and auto-stash them (SPIRAL_AUTO_STASH=true) or abort with a clear actionable message.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh detects uncommitted changes before Phase I using git status --porcelain
With SPIRAL_AUTO_STASH=true: changes are stashed, Phase I runs, stash is popped after
With SPIRAL_AUTO_STASH=false (default): Phase I is skipped with an actionable message
Stash pop failure is logged as a warning and does not crash the run
A bats test exercises both the stash and abort paths
technicalNotes: git stash push --include-untracked -m spiral-auto-stash-iter-N
Pop in a trap so it runs even on interrupt
SPIRAL_AUTO_STASH documented in spiral.config.example.sh

userStories.title: Rotate ralph-run.log when size exceeds SPIRAL_LOG_MAX_MB
userStories.description: ralph-run.log grows unbounded across runs. Long projects accumulate hundreds of MB. Add automatic rotation: when the log exceeds SPIRAL_LOG_MAX_MB (default 50 MB), rename to .log.1 and start fresh. Keep at most SPIRAL_LOG_KEEP_ROTATIONS (default 3) old files.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh checks log file size before each iteration
When size exceeds limit: rename .log to .log.1, .log.1 to .log.2, etc.
Files beyond SPIRAL_LOG_KEEP_ROTATIONS are deleted
A rotation notice is written as the first line of the new log
Both config vars documented in spiral.config.example.sh
technicalNotes: Portable size check via python3 os.path.getsize()
Rotate at iteration start before appending
On Windows, close and reopen the fd around the rename

userStories.title: Add Claude API reachability probe to pre-flight check and spiral-doctor
userStories.description: Spiral discovers Claude is unreachable only after a full phase timeout, wasting minutes. Add a fast 5-second connectivity probe to startup pre-flight and spiral-doctor. Fail immediately with exit code 5 and a clear message if the API does not respond.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral-doctor reports PASS/FAIL for Claude API reachable check
spiral.sh pre-flight includes the same probe (skippable with SPIRAL_SKIP_API_CHECK=true)
The probe uses a 5-second timeout and does not count against SPIRAL_RESEARCH_RETRIES
Failure exits with code 5 and a clear message
Probe is skipped in --dry-run mode
technicalNotes: Use timeout 5 claude --version or a minimal 1-token completion
Add check_claude_api() to lib/spiral_doctor.sh
Also verify ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is non-empty before any network call

userStories.title: Scan for duplicate story IDs in prd.json at startup and abort or deduplicate
userStories.description: Parallel merges or manual edits can produce duplicate story IDs. When jq processes duplicates, one silently overwrites the other causing data loss. Add a startup scan: strict mode (default) exits with a conflict list; lenient mode (SPIRAL_DEDUP_IDS=lenient) keeps the passes:true entry and drops the duplicate.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh scans prd.json for duplicate IDs at startup
Strict mode (default): duplicate IDs cause exit 1 with a printed conflict list
Lenient mode: keep passes:true entry, drop duplicate, log warning
Result is written back atomically
A bats test covers both modes with a pre-seeded duplicate prd.json
technicalNotes: Use jq group_by to detect duplicates
Run after config load, before prd_stats()
Write events to spiral_events.jsonl

userStories.title: Replace bare exit numbers in spiral.sh with named exit code constants
userStories.description: Spiral exits with 0 or generic 1 from most paths. CI pipelines cannot distinguish all-done from API-crashed from cost-ceiling-hit. Define named readonly constants (EXIT_DONE=0, EXIT_MAX_ITERS=2, EXIT_COST_CEILING=3, EXIT_CYCLE=4, EXIT_API_DOWN=5, EXIT_PRD_CORRUPT=6) and use them everywhere. Document in --help and README.
acceptanceCriteria: Named constants defined near top of spiral.sh with readonly
All exit points use constants not bare numbers
spiral.sh --help lists exit codes with descriptions
README.md has an exit codes table
A bats test triggers each path and asserts the correct code
technicalNotes: Use values 2-9 to stay in the conventional range
Audit all existing exit 1 calls and remap each
Keep EXIT_DONE=0 and EXIT_ERR=1 to match POSIX convention

userStories.title: Run Phase R and Phase T in parallel to halve story-preparation wall time
userStories.description: Phase R (60-300s) and Phase T (30-60s) are completely independent but run sequentially. Launching them as background bash jobs and awaiting both halves story-preparation latency every iteration with no quality tradeoff.
acceptanceCriteria: Phase R and Phase T run as background jobs awaited before Phase M
If either fails, the other continues and its output is treated as empty
The banner shows combined wall time as max(R,T) rather than R+T
Checkpoint records R and T independently so resume skips only what finished
A bats test with mock scripts confirms they run simultaneously
technicalNotes: run_phase_r & PID_R=$!; run_phase_t & PID_T=$!; wait $PID_R; RC_R=$?; wait $PID_T; RC_T=$?
Redirect each phase stdout to a dedicated section to avoid log interleaving
Checkpoint needs phase_r_done and phase_t_done as separate fields

userStories.title: Skip Phase V test suite when Phase I produced zero new story passes
userStories.description: Phase V runs the full test suite after every Phase I even when all stories were retried or skipped and nothing changed. Snapshot the passed-story count before Phase I; if it has not increased after, skip Phase V and go directly to Phase C.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh counts passed stories before and after Phase I
If the count did not increase: Phase V is skipped with a logged message
If the count increased: Phase V runs normally
SPIRAL_FORCE_VALIDATE=true bypasses the skip for CI
Skip is recorded in _checkpoint.json and _iteration_summary.json
A bats test confirms Phase V is skipped when Phase I produces no passes
technicalNotes: PASSES_BEFORE=$(jq count) before Phase I; PASSES_AFTER=$(same) after
Works in parallel-worker mode because prd.json is the shared source of truth

userStories.title: Emit OTel GenAI agent spans per story phase using semconv
userStories.description: SPIRAL already emits JSONL telemetry events but not standard OpenTelemetry traces. Instrument each phase (R, T, I, V, M, G) as an OTel child span under a root invoke_agent span, setting the required GenAI semantic convention attributes (gen_ai.agent.name, gen_ai.agent.id, gen_ai.operation.name, gen_ai.system) so traces are consumable by any OTLP-compatible backend. This enables cross-tool observability and cost attribution without vendor lock-in.
acceptanceCriteria: Each story execution produces a root span named invoke_agent spiral with child spans per phase
Spans include gen_ai.agent.name=spiral, gen_ai.operation.name matching phase letter, gen_ai.system=anthropic
Token counts from Claude responses are recorded as gen_ai.usage.input_tokens and gen_ai.usage.output_tokens span attributes
Spans export via OTLP HTTP to OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT when env var is set; silently no-ops when unset
A Jaeger or Zipkin trace confirms parent/child relationship across all 6 phases
technicalNotes: Use opentelemetry-sdk Python package; wrap existing phase invocations in lib/ Python modules
OTel GenAI agent span spec: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-agent-spans/
Span kind should be CLIENT for cross-process agents, INTERNAL when in-process
Correlate with existing trace_id / span_id fields already written to JSONL event log

userStories.title: Import user stories from GitHub Issues via GraphQL API
userStories.description: Teams who track work in GitHub Issues must manually copy stories into prd.json. Add a spiral import-github command that queries a repo's issues via the GitHub GraphQL API, maps issue title/body/labels to prd.json story fields, and appends them with state=pending. This eliminates dual-entry and keeps SPIRAL in sync with the team's issue tracker.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral import-github --repo owner/repo --label spiral pulls all open issues with the given label
Issue title maps to story title; body maps to description; milestone maps to priority
Duplicate detection: issues whose title already exists in prd.json are skipped with a warning
Paginated fetching handles repos with >100 matching issues using GraphQL cursor pagination
Dry-run mode (--dry-run) prints the stories that would be added without modifying prd.json
Requires GITHUB_TOKEN env var; exits with error code 1 and clear message if missing
technicalNotes: GitHub GraphQL Issues query: https://docs.github.com/en/graphql/reference/objects#issue
Use gh CLI's --paginate flag or python-gql library for cursor-based pagination
Map GitHub labels to priority: priority:critical→critical, priority:high→high, else medium

userStories.title: Pre-flight cross-story file conflict detection using git merge-tree
userStories.description: When SPIRAL assigns multiple stories to parallel workers, stories that touch the same files will produce merge conflicts at Phase M, wasting compute. Before launching workers for a batch, run a read-only git merge-tree simulation between each pair of active worktree branches to detect overlapping file edits early and requeue one story of each conflicting pair to the next batch.
acceptanceCriteria: Before each parallel batch launch, all story pair combinations are checked with git merge-tree --write-tree
If merge-tree exits non-zero for a pair, the lower-priority story is removed from the current batch and its state is set back to pending
The conflict detection result is written to a conflict-log.jsonl entry with story pair, conflicting file paths, and batch number
Detection completes within 5 seconds for batches of up to 8 stories
A --skip-conflict-preflight flag bypasses the check for debugging purposes
technicalNotes: git merge-tree --write-tree <base> <branchA> <branchB> exits non-zero and lists conflicts to stdout
Base commit for three-way merge is the common ancestor: git merge-base branchA branchB
Conflict preflight is distinct from the existing merge conflict detection story which operates post-implementation

userStories.title: Detect and block prompt injection patterns in prd.json story fields
userStories.description: prd.json is readable by external contributors and could contain adversarial instructions in description or acceptanceCriteria fields. Before passing story content to Claude, scan text fields through an injection pattern detector and abort the phase with a security error if injection signatures are found, protecting the SPIRAL agent from indirect prompt injection.
acceptanceCriteria: A scan_for_injection(text) function checks for OWASP LLM01:2025 injection patterns before each LLM call
Detected patterns include: ignore/disregard previous instructions, system prompt override attempts, jailbreak tokens (DAN, STAN), and base64-encoded suspicious payloads
When injection is detected, the story is set to failed with reason=security_block and a WARN entry is appended to the security audit log (security-audit.jsonl)
A --allow-unsafe-stories flag bypasses scanning (requires explicit user opt-in)
Unit tests cover at least 10 known injection variants from OWASP Top 10 for LLMs 2025
technicalNotes: OWASP LLM01:2025 Prompt Injection: https://genai.owasp.org/llmrisk/llm01-prompt-injection/
LLM Guard PromptInjection scanner uses a fine-tuned classifier: https://github.com/protectai/llm-guard
Lightweight alternative: regex-based pattern list covering common jailbreak phrases
NIST AI RMF and ISO 42001 now mandate prompt injection controls for production AI agents

userStories.title: Sanitize LLM output before writing to disk to block indirect injection
userStories.description: SPIRAL writes Claude's generated code and test files directly to the worktree. A compromised upstream package or injected web content fetched during Phase R could cause Claude to emit shell escape sequences, git config overwrites, or malicious hook scripts. Add an output sanitizer that validates generated file paths stay within the worktree and strips ANSI escape codes and null bytes before disk writes.
acceptanceCriteria: All file write operations from Phase I validate the target path resolves within the story's worktree directory (path traversal prevention)
ANSI escape sequences and null bytes are stripped from all LLM-generated file content before writing
Attempts to write to .git/ paths are blocked and logged to security-audit.jsonl
Attempts to write executable files outside src/ and tests/ directories require SPIRAL_ALLOW_EXEC_WRITES=true
Sanitization does not break legitimate code generation (test with a Python file containing ANSI strings as data)
technicalNotes: Path traversal prevention: use os.path.realpath() and assert path.startswith(worktree_root)
OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet: https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/LLM_Prompt_Injection_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html
Strip ANSI: re.sub(r'\x1b\[[0-9;]*m', '', content); strip null: content.replace('\x00', '')

userStories.title: Record per-phase LLM token metrics as OTel GenAI metrics
userStories.description: Complement span-level tracing with OTel metrics for token throughput and latency so operators can set alerts on token budgets without parsing spans. Emit the four mandatory GenAI metrics (gen_ai.client.token.usage, gen_ai.client.operation.duration, gen_ai.server.request.duration, gen_ai.server.ttft) as histograms/counters with story_id and phase labels attached as attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: gen_ai.client.token.usage counter increments by prompt+completion tokens after each LLM call
gen_ai.client.operation.duration histogram records wall-clock ms per LLM call
Metrics are exported via OTLP when OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set
Prometheus scrape endpoint /metrics available when SPIRAL_PROM_PORT env var is set
Dashboard (spiral-ui) shows a per-story token burn sparkline sourced from these metrics
technicalNotes: OTel GenAI metrics spec: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-metrics/
Use opentelemetry-sdk MeterProvider with PrometheusExporter for local scrape endpoint
Attribute token counts from Claude's usage field in API response body
dependencies: Emit OTel GenAI agent spans per story phase using semconv

userStories.title: Add semantic-release automated version bump on spiral completion
userStories.description: When a SPIRAL run finishes with at least one story merged, automatically determine the next SemVer version from conventional commit history since the last tag, create a git tag, and write a CHANGELOG section. This removes manual tagging and ensures every successful run produces a traceable artifact. Uses python-semantic-release so no Node.js runtime is required.
acceptanceCriteria: Running spiral.sh with SPIRAL_AUTO_RELEASE=true triggers version calculation after Phase M completes all stories
Version bump follows conventional commits: feat→minor, fix/perf→patch, BREAKING CHANGE footer→major
A git tag vX.Y.Z is created and pushed when SPIRAL_GIT_PUSH=true
CHANGELOG.md is updated with a new section listing story titles and commit SHAs
If no releasable commits exist, no tag is created and a warning is logged
technicalNotes: python-semantic-release docs: https://python-semantic-release.readthedocs.io/
SPIRAL already enforces conventional commits; add semantic_release.toml config pointing at main branch
Integrate as a post-run hook in spiral.sh after the final merge phase completes

userStories.title: Implement model benchmark harness to compare worker model outputs
userStories.description: SPIRAL supports a model routing table but has no way to measure which model produces higher-quality story implementations. Add a benchmark mode that runs the same story through two configured models in separate worktrees, scores both outputs against acceptance criteria using an LLM judge, and writes results to benchmark-results.jsonl. This enables data-driven model routing decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: spiral.sh --benchmark <story-id> --models modelA,modelB executes both models against the same story
Each model run is isolated in its own worktree and branch
An LLM judge (configurable, defaults to claude-3-5-haiku) scores each output on a 1-5 rubric across correctness, test pass rate, and code style
benchmark-results.jsonl records model name, story id, score per dimension, token cost, and wall time
spiral-ui benchmark tab visualises side-by-side scores for all benchmarked stories
technicalNotes: LLM-as-judge evaluation pattern documented by DeepEval: https://docs.confident-ai.com/docs/evaluation-introduction
Score dimensions align with SPIRAL acceptance criteria fields already in prd.json
Use existing worktree isolation infrastructure; add a benchmark state to story lifecycle

userStories.title: Track actual vs estimated complexity and surface calibration report
userStories.description: Stories in prd.json carry estimatedComplexity (small/medium/large) but SPIRAL never measures whether estimates were accurate. After each story completes, record actual phase durations and pass/fail count to a calibration log. Periodically compute a calibration score and surface it in spiral-ui so users can improve future estimates.
acceptanceCriteria: On story completion, a calibration record is appended to calibration.jsonl with story_id, estimated_complexity, actual_duration_s, phase_retries, and passed boolean
spiral-ui shows a calibration panel with median actual duration per complexity tier (small/medium/large)
When actual duration exceeds the median for a tier by 2x, the story is flagged as underestimated in the dashboard
A calibration summary is written to the run report after each full SPIRAL run
spiral --calibration-report prints the calibration table to stdout
technicalNotes: Agile velocity feedback loop pattern: use rolling window of last 20 completed stories for median calculation
Complexity tiers can be mapped to rough hour buckets: small=<30m, medium=30m-2h, large=>2h
Integrate with existing results.tsv output so no new file format is strictly required

userStories.title: Implement SPIRAL plugin system with hook-based extension points
userStories.description: SPIRAL has per-phase hooks in config but no formal plugin API for third-party extensions. Introduce a plugins/ directory convention where each subdirectory is a plugin containing a plugin.toml manifest and hook scripts. SPIRAL scans plugins/ on startup, validates manifests, and invokes hook scripts at declared lifecycle points. This enables community extensions without forking spiral.sh.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/my-plugin/plugin.toml with name, version, hooks[] fields is discovered and loaded on startup
Supported hook points: pre-phase, post-phase, post-story, post-run, pre-merge
